summary: Large decompilation/matching batch across fighter, item, stage, menu, game-mode, and library code. The decomp.dev report credited the PR with +9176 matched bytes, 69 new 100% matches, and 14 unmatched-item improvements, with most work replacing placeholder `/// #...` stubs with C implementations and cleaning related prototypes/headers. Review initially requested fixing regressions against both `master` and a pre-merge commit using `ninja baseline`/`ninja changes`; the final remaining regressions were accepted as more or less false positives.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed functions across 68 files, with 1437 added and 217 deleted lines in the slice metadata.

Matched several fighter-common routines in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, including `fn_800D86E0`, `fn_800DA2B0`, `fn_800DA4FC`, and `ftCo_800DA698`, covering item-scope end, catch-wait, catch-attack, and catch-cut transitions.
Implemented `ftCo_80094098` in `ftCo_Guard.c`, using a fighter part joint lookup through `fp->parts[fp->ft_data->x8->x11].joint`, `lb_8000B1CC`, and `inlineB0(fp)`.
Added Crazy Hand routines in `ftCh_Init.c`, including `fn_80156F6C`, `ftCh_Init_8015A2B0`, `ftCh_Init_8015A3F4`, `ftCh_Init_8015A560`, and `ftCh_Fail_Anim`.

Added Kirby copy-ability and hat-related implementations: `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040`, collision transitions for Captain/Ganondorf-style neutral special variants, and `ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C` special-N index selection.
Updated headers to expose newly implemented or newly referenced symbols and to correct signatures, including changing `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040` from `UNK_RET` to `void`.
Touched many item and stage files with small decomp matches or data/prototype adjustments, including old Kuri, Tincle, Dosei, Hitodeman, Ness yoyo, Zako generator, Mute City, Zebes Route, and others.

The bot report recorded 69 new matches and 14 improvements, including examples such as `fn_800259EC`, `fn_80025A98`, `ftCo_800DA698`, `ftKb_CaSpecialN_Coll`, `ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C`, `grZakoGenerator_801CA394`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040`.
smart_moves: Used local `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` blocks only around functions that needed codegen control, instead of globally changing compilation behavior.

Preserved register/codegen-sensitive patterns in Kirby hat loaders, including the documented self-assignment style and switching to `gobj->user_data` plus adjusted `PAD_STACK(8)` where needed for matching.
Kept address comments on prototypes and function declarations, making the header changes easier to audit against the map and matching reports.
Used numeric motion IDs with inline comments in `ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C` where enum use was known to be unsafe because of the documented `ftKb_MS` / `ftCo_MS_Count` mismatch.

Matched transition routines by preserving callbacks and side effects such as `pre_hitlag_cb`, `post_hitlag_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`, `accessory1_cb`, velocity fields, and `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments.
Ran or responded to decomp.dev / ninja comparison tooling rather than relying only on local function-level matches.
decomp_lessons: For large matching PRs, compare against both current `master` and the pre-merge baseline; a PR can improve matched bytes while still regressing previously matched functions.
Small helper access differences such as `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` versus `gobj->user_data` can matter for matching and register allocation.
`PAD_STACK` and `dont_inline` pragmas remain practical tools for isolated codegen fixes, but should be scoped narrowly.
When an enum is known to have a count or offset mismatch, numeric IDs with explicit comments can be safer than forcing misleading enum names.
Header cleanup is part of matching: adding address-ordered prototypes, correcting return types, and moving declarations from ad hoc positions reduces future mismatch and include friction.
Bot-reported fuzzy-match regressions may be false positives, but they still need explicit review and documentation before merge.
Large cross-system batches should preserve function addresses and subsystem prefixes so future search can connect reports, headers, and source implementations.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-based names such as `fn_800D86E0`, `fn_80156F6C`, and `fn_8024FBA4`.
Subsystem-qualified names were preserved where known, such as `ftCo_`, `ftKb_`, `ftCh_`, `grZakoGenerator_`, `grMuteCity_`, `mnName_`, and item file prefixes.
Headers use address comments of the form `/* 0D86E0 */` before prototypes.
Unknown signatures remain marked with `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` until evidence is stronger.

Motion-state constants use established names where reliable, such as `ftCo_MS_ItemScopeEnd`, `ftCo_MS_CatchWait`, `ftCo_MS_CatchAttack`, and `ftCo_MS_CatchCut`; numeric constants were retained where enum definitions were suspect.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` around sensitive functions including `fn_800D86E0`, `ftCo_CatchAttack_Anim`, `ftCo_800DA698`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F130C`.
Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)` in functions where stack shape affected matching, including fighter catch-cut and Kirby special-N code.
Used `gobj->user_data` directly in some functions to influence register allocation.
Preserved an explicit self-assignment idiom in Kirby hat-loading routines as noted by the in-code comment.
Maintained exact `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` parameter ordering and float literals such as `0.0F`, `1.0F`, and `0.0f` to match generated code.
Used `ninja baseline` and `ninja changes` as the review workflow for identifying regressions against `master` and a pre-merge commit.
Accepted a small set of final fuzzy-match changes only after reviewer assessment that they were mostly false positives.

review_feedback.feedback: Requested fixing all regressions relative to both `master` and pre-merge commit `2ecbc73630c5c584dc369367e3dd23d51b38bfff`, using `ninja baseline` and `ninja changes`.
Approved after noting the last regressions were more or less false positives.
Final comment said changes against `master` were good to go, while listing a few remaining fuzzy-match deltas versus the pre-merge commit.

follow_up_queries: Find PRs that use `#pragma dont_inline` plus `PAD_STACK` for matching fighter functions.
Find later fixes for the `ftKb_MS` enum and `ftCo_MS_Count` mismatch mentioned in `ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C`.
Search for follow-up PRs touching `ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0` or `ftKb_SpecialN_800F130C` after the initial regression review.
Compare later cleanup of address-based `fn_800D86E0`, `fn_80156F6C`, and other newly matched symbols for real names.
Look for additional PRs where decomp.dev fuzzy-match regressions were accepted as false positives.
